rcu.c(3) Library Functions Manual rcu.c(3)

NAME

rcu.c

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct int_field_ctx_t
Context for integer field updates in RCU updater callback. struct bool_field_ctx_t
Context for boolean field updates in RCU updater callback. struct string_field_ctx_t
Context for string field updates in RCU updater callback. struct double_field_ctx_t
Context for double field updates in RCU updater callback.

Macros


#define MAX_DEFERRED_FREES 64
Deferred free list for old options structs.

Functions


__attribute__ ((constructor))
Constructor to initialize fork handlers at startup. asciichat_error_t options_state_init (void)
asciichat_error_t options_state_set (const options_t *opts)
void options_state_destroy (void)
void options_cleanup_schema (void)
const options_t * options_get (void)
asciichat_error_t options_set_int (const char *field_name, int value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_bool (const char *field_name, bool value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_string (const char *field_name, const char *value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_double (const char *field_name, double value)

Macro Definition Documentation

#define MAX_DEFERRED_FREES 64

Deferred free list for old options structs. We can't immediately free old structs because readers might still be using them. Simple strategy: Keep old pointers in a list, free them at shutdown.

Future improvements:

  • Hazard pointers: Track which structs readers are using
  • Epoch-based reclamation: Free after grace period
  • Reference counting: Free when no readers

Current approach: Never free until shutdown (acceptable for rare updates)

Definition at line 275 of file rcu.c.

Function Documentation

__attribute__ ((constructor) )

Constructor to initialize fork handlers at startup. This is called before main() by the linker, ensuring fork handlers are registered early.

Definition at line 352 of file rcu.c.

352                                                                                   {
353   // Register the child handler that will be called after fork() in the child process
354   pthread_atfork(NULL, NULL, options_rcu_atfork_child);
355 }








void
  options_cleanup_schema (void )


Definition at line 475 of file rcu.c.


475                                   {
476   config_schema_destroy();
477 }






References config_schema_destroy().


Referenced by main().






const
  options_t * options_get (void )


Definition at line 479 of file rcu.c.


479                                    {
480   // Lock-free read with acquire semantics
481   // Guarantees we see all writes made before the pointer was published
482   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
483 
484   // If options not yet published or after destruction, return safe static default.
485   // This is a critical fallback that:
486   // 1. Allows early startup code (before options_state_init) to work safely
487   // 2. Allows atexit handlers and cleanup code to call GET_OPTION() safely
488   // 3. Never crashes due to NULL pointer access
489   // 4. Provides sensible defaults for all option fields
490   //
491   // The static default options are:
492   // - Allocated once at startup (static storage)
493   // - Never freed (outlives all dynamically allocated options)
494   // - Thread-safe to read (immutable const data)
495   // - Complete with all OPT_*_DEFAULT values matching options_t_new()
496   if (!current) {
497     return (const options_t *)&g_default_options;
498   }
499 
500   return current;
501 }






References atomic_ptr_load().


Referenced by acds_main(), client_crypto_init(),
    client_main(), discovery_session_process(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), main(),
    nat_detect_quality(), server_crypto_handshake(),
    session_settings_from_options(), terminal_screen_render(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






asciichat_error_t
  options_set_bool (const char * field_name, bool value)


Definition at line 703 of file rcu.c.


703                                                                        {
704   if (!field_name) {
705     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");
706     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
707   }
708 
709   // Validate field exists
710   if (strcmp(field_name, "no_compress") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "encode_audio") != 0 &&
711       strcmp(field_name, "flip_x") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "flip_y") != 0 &&
712       strcmp(field_name, "test_pattern") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "no_audio_mixer") != 0 &&
713       strcmp(field_name, "show_capabilities") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "force_utf8") != 0 &&
714       strcmp(field_name, "audio_enabled") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "audio_analysis_enabled") != 0 &&
715       strcmp(field_name, "audio_no_playback") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "stretch") != 0 &&
716       strcmp(field_name, "snapshot_mode") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "strip_ansi") != 0 &&
717       strcmp(field_name, "quiet") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "encrypt_enabled") != 0 &&
718       strcmp(field_name, "no_encrypt") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery") != 0 &&
719       strcmp(field_name, "discovery_expose_ip") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery_insecure") != 0 &&
720       strcmp(field_name, "webrtc") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "lan_discovery") != 0 &&
721       strcmp(field_name, "no_mdns_advertise") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "prefer_webrtc") != 0 &&
722       strcmp(field_name, "no_webrtc") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_skip_stun") != 0 &&
723       strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_disable_turn") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "enable_upnp") != 0 &&
724       strcmp(field_name, "require_server_identity") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "require_client_identity") != 0 &&
725       strcmp(field_name, "require_server_verify") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "require_client_verify") != 0 &&
726       strcmp(field_name, "palette_custom_set") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "media_loop") != 0 &&
727       strcmp(field_name, "media_from_stdin") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "auto_width") != 0 &&
728       strcmp(field_name, "auto_height") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "splash_screen") != 0 &&
729       strcmp(field_name, "status_screen") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "matrix_rain") != 0) {
730     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown boolean field: %s", field_name);
731     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
732   }
733 
734   bool_field_ctx_t ctx = {.field_name = field_name, .value = value};
735   return options_update(bool_field_updater, &ctx);
736 }

asciichat_error_t
  options_set_double (const char * field_name, double value)


Definition at line 836 of file rcu.c.


836                                                                            {
837   if (!field_name) {
838     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");
839     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
840   }
841 
842   // Normalize option names to internal field names
843   const char *internal_name = field_name;
844   if (strcmp(field_name, "microphone-volume") == 0 || strcmp(field_name, "ivolume") == 0) {
845     internal_name = "microphone_sensitivity";
846   } else if (strcmp(field_name, "speakers-volume") == 0 || strcmp(field_name, "volume") == 0) {
847     internal_name = "speakers_volume";
848   }
849 
850   if (strcmp(internal_name, "snapshot_delay") != 0 && strcmp(internal_name, "microphone_sensitivity") != 0 &&
851       strcmp(internal_name, "speakers_volume") != 0) {
852     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown double field: %s", field_name);
853     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
854   }
855 
856   double_field_ctx_t ctx = {.field_name = internal_name, .value = value};
857   return options_update(double_field_updater, &ctx);
858 }

790     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");

802       strcmp(field_name, "encrypt_keyfile") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "server_key") != 0 &&
803       strcmp(field_name, "client_keys") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery_server") != 0 &&
804       strcmp(field_name, "discovery_service_key") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery_database_path") != 0 &&
805       strcmp(field_name, "log_file") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "media_file") != 0 &&
806       strcmp(field_name, "palette_custom") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "stun_servers") != 0 &&
807       strcmp(field_name, "turn_servers") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "turn_username") != 0 &&
808       strcmp(field_name, "turn_credential") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "turn_secret") != 0 &&
809       strcmp(field_name, "session_string") != 0) {
810     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown string field: %s", field_name);
811     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
812   }
813

void
  options_state_destroy (void )


Definition at line 444 of file rcu.c.


444                                  {
445   // Check if initialized using lifecycle
446   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
447     return;
448   }
449 
450   /* Shutdown lifecycle to mark as no longer initialized */
451   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
452 
453   // Get current options pointer
454   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
455 
456   // Clear the atomic pointer (prevent further reads)
457   atomic_ptr_store(&g_options, NULL);
458 
459   // Free current struct
460   SAFE_FREE(current);
461 
462   // Free all deferred structs
463   deferred_free_all();
464 
465   // Cleanup schema (frees all dynamically allocated config strings)
466   config_schema_destroy();
467 
468   // Destroy dynamically created mutexes
469   mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
470   mutex_destroy(&g_deferred_free_mutex);
471 
472   log_debug("Options state shutdown complete");
473 }






References atomic_ptr_load(), atomic_ptr_store(),
    config_schema_destroy(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), mutex_destroy(), and
  SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    client_cleanup(), main(), and mirror_cleanup().






asciichat_error_t
  options_state_init (void )


Definition at line 362 of file rcu.c.


362                                            {
363   // Detect fork: after fork(), child process must reinitialize
364   pid_t current_pid = platform_get_pid();
365   if ((lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle) || g_init_pid != -1) && g_init_pid != current_pid) {
366     log_debug("Detected fork in options_state_init (parent PID %d, child PID %d) - resetting lifecycle", g_init_pid,
367               current_pid);
368     /* Reset lifecycle to allow reinitialization after fork */
369     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
370     g_init_pid = current_pid;
371   }
372 
373   // Check if already initialized in this process using lifecycle
374   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle) && g_init_pid == current_pid) {
375     log_warn("Options state already initialized");
376     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
377   }
378 
379   /* Use lifecycle to serialize initialization */
380   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_options_lifecycle, "options")) {
381     /* Another thread is initializing or already initialized */
382     if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
383       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
384     }
385     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to acquire options initialization lock");
386   }
387 
388   // Initialize write mutex
389   if (mutex_init(&g_options_write_mutex, "options_write") != 0) {
390     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
391     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize options write mutex");
392   }
393 
394   // Initialize deferred free mutex
395   if (mutex_init(&g_deferred_free_mutex, "deferred_free") != 0) {
396     mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
397     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
398     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize deferred free mutex");
399   }
400 
401   // Allocate initial options struct (will be populated later by options_state_populate_from_globals)
402   options_t *initial_opts = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(options_t), options_t *);
403   if (!initial_opts) {
404     mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
405     mutex_destroy(&g_deferred_free_mutex);
406     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
407     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate initial options struct");
408   }
409 
410   // Initialize all defaults using options_t_new()
411   *initial_opts = options_t_new();
412 
413   // Publish initial struct (release semantics - make all fields visible to readers)
414   atomic_ptr_store(&g_options, initial_opts);
415 
416   g_init_pid = current_pid; // Record PID to detect fork later
417   log_dev("Options state initialized with RCU pattern (PID %d)", current_pid);
418 
419   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
420 }






References atomic_ptr_store(), lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), lifecycle_shutdown(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), options_t_new(),
    platform_get_pid(), SAFE_MALLOC, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by __attribute__(), and
  options_init().






asciichat_error_t
  options_state_set (const options_t * opts)


Definition at line 422 of file rcu.c.


422                                                            {
423   if (!opts) {
424     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "opts is NULL");
425   }
426 
427   // Check initialization state using lifecycle
428   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
429     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Options state not initialized (call options_state_init first)");
430   }
431 
432   // Get current struct (should be the initial zero-initialized one during startup)
433   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
434 
435   // Copy provided options into current struct
436   // Note: No need for RCU update here since this is called during initialization
437   // before any reader threads exist
438   memcpy(current, opts, sizeof(options_t));
439 
440   log_debug("Options state set from parsed struct");
441   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
442 }






References atomic_ptr_load(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by config_load_and_apply(), and
    options_init().
